profilePicker.title: Choose a profile
profilePicker.subtitle: Each profile has its own settings, holdings, and report history.

empty.title: No profiles yet
empty.body: Build your first investment profile — risk band, allocation targets, accounts, watchlist. Takes about two minutes.

modal.startFreshHint: Step-by-step wizard. Builds a complete profile.yaml with validated inputs.
modal.cloneHint: Copy an existing profile's settings and holdings as a starting point.

clone.title: Clone profile
clone.name: Name
clone.errorPickSource: Pick a source profile to clone from.

wizard.title: Build your investment profile
wizard.subtitle: Answers feed directly into the monthly-report prompt. You can change anything later from the Settings page.

step1.title: Who is this profile for?
step1.hint: Drives tax framing and prompt context. Belgium unlocks the prebuilt Belgian-tax block (Reynders threshold, accumulating IE/LU ETF preference, TOB minimization). Other residencies fall back to a generic prompt — Claude will look up your tax rules.
step1.taxNonBelgiumNote: Belgian-specific tax block will be omitted. Claude will look up your local tax rules at report time.
step1.citizenshipHint: Affects context only. Add or remove chips as needed.
step1.poolSizeBandHint: Used to right-size trade recommendations and TOB sensitivity.

step2.title: Risk & horizon
step2.hint: Shapes the default growth/defensive split on the next step.

step3.title: Cashflow
step3.hint: Section 4 of the monthly report deploys the midpoint of the contribution range. Pick a band you can sustain.
step3.cashBufferHint: Buffer = expenses × months. The report flags any shortfall.

step4.title: Allocation targets
step4.hint: Drives the drift table in section 2 of the report. Each group must sum to 100.
step4.driftToleranceHint: Section 2 only flags drifts that exceed this for rebalance.

step5.title: Accounts
step5.hint: Each account becomes a soft-cap target the report tracks. We'll prefill an empty cash row per account in holdings.csv so the Holdings page opens with structure.
step5.name: Name

step6.title: Watchlist & macro
step6.hint: Tickers fetched at report time via yfinance. Use Yahoo Finance ticker shape (e.g. VWCE.DE, EURBRL=X).
step6.brazilMacroHint: Pulled from BCB SGS series. Useful when you hold BRL or Tesouro Direto.

step7.title: Constraints, reporting & review
step7.hint: Hard rules the report must obey. Add or remove chips.
step7.belgianBlockBody: Including the standard Belgian-tax rules: prefer accumulating IE/LU ETFs, avoid bond ETFs >10% interest-bearing, prefer passive broad-index, TOB minimization note.

settings.subtitle: Profile policy for {{name}}

apiKey.statusSet: A key is stored on this device (encrypted via the OS keychain).
apiKey.statusMissing: No key stored. Reports won't run until one is saved.

settings.ccyMismatch: Currencies sum to {{total}}%, not 100%. (Treated as targets, not strict constraints.)
settings.accountsEmpty: No accounts yet. Add one to feed suggestions into the Holdings editor.
settings.advancedYamlHint: Edits here are the source of truth. Form fields above re-render on save.

holdings.appended_one: Appended {{count}} row — click Save to keep
holdings.appended_other: Appended {{count}} rows — click Save to keep

table.empty: No holdings yet. Add your first row below.

reports.customSubtitle: Add specific considerations to focus this report on. The standard monthly template still applies; your text fills the User considerations section.
reports.customPlaceholder: e.g., Focus on BRL hedging strategies and evaluate Tesouro Direto allocation given the latest SELIC move.
reports.emptyBody: No reports yet — click Generate this month to create the first one.

error.pdfRender: Report generated, but PDF render failed

hero.pitch2: Undecided on which penny stock to put your money on?
hero.subline: Skip the charts. Ask an AI that reads live market data—answers in seconds.
hero.placeholder: I have 10000 euros, which ETF should I buy this month?
hero.errorGeneric: Cowbot couldn't answer that. Try again.

profile.body: Tell Cowbot what you actually own, what you're saving for, and what you can stomach losing. With a full investor profile it stops guessing and starts reasoning — scenario by scenario, currency by currency, year by year.
profile.cardEyebrow: What you get with a profile
profile.bullet2: Set risk tolerance, horizon, and currency
profile.bullet3: Run scenario reports — drawdowns, FX shocks, retirement glide

footer.tagline: Cowboy Investor is software, not a registered investment adviser. Outputs are educational. Decisions, and their consequences, are yours.
footer.disclaimer: Investing involves risk, including the possible loss of principal. The information here is generated by software and may contain inaccuracies. Nothing on this site is an offer to buy or sell any security.

list.title: Recent questions
list.empty: Ask Cowbot a question to start your history.

detail.question: Your question
detail.notFound: Advice not found or no longer accessible.
